title: Theory of Computation - Context Free Grammar

questions.question:
Let \( G = (V, T, S, P) \) be a context-free grammar such that every one of its productions is of the form \( A \rightarrow ν \), with \( |ν| = k > 1 \). The derivation tree for any string \( W \in L (G) \) has a height such that



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | August 2016 | Part 3 | Question: 57

Which sentence can be generated by \( S\rightarrow d/bA, A\rightarrow d/ccA \) :



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | December 2005 | Part 2 | Question: 4

Contex-free Grammar (CFG) can be recognized by

(A) Finite state automata
(B) 2-way linear bounded automata
(C) push down automata
(D) both (B) and (C)


Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | December 2009 | Part 2 | Question: 34

The context free grammar for the language:

\( L=\{a^n b^m \mid n \leq m + 3, n \geq 0, m \geq 0 \} \) is



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | December 2013 | Part 2 | Question: 27
options: \( S \rightarrow aaa \quad A; A \rightarrow aAb \mid B, B \rightarrow Bb \mid \lambda \)
\( S \rightarrow aaaA\mid \lambda, A \rightarrow aAb \mid B, B \rightarrow Bb \mid \lambda \)
\( S \rightarrow aaaA \mid aa A \mid \lambda, A \rightarrow aAb \mid B, B \rightarrow Bb \mid \lambda \)
\( S \rightarrow aaaA \mid aa \quad A \mid aA \mid \lambda, A \rightarrow aAb \mid B, B \rightarrow Bb \mid \lambda \)
questions.correct_answer: \( S \rightarrow aaaA \mid aa \quad A \mid aA \mid \lambda, A \rightarrow aAb \mid B, B \rightarrow Bb \mid \lambda \)

questions.question:
The following Context-Free Grammar (CFG) :

\( S \rightarrow aB | bA \)

\( A \rightarrow a | as | bAA \)

\( B \rightarrow b | bs | aBB \) 

will generate



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | December 2014 | Part 2 | Question: 35
options: Odd numbers of \( a's \) and odd numbers of \( b's \)
Even numbers of \( a's \) and even numbers of \( b's \)

questions.question:
Let \( G= (V,T,S,P) \) be a context-free grammer such that every one of its productions is of the form \( A\rightarrow v \), with \( \mid  v \mid=K> 1 \). The derivation tree for any \( W \in L(G) \) has a height \( h \) such that 



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | January 2017 | Part 3 | Question: 22

Which one of the following is not a Greibach Normal form grammar?

(i)

\( S \rightarrow a \mid bA \mid aA \mid bB \)

\( A \rightarrow a \)

\( B \rightarrow b \)
(ii)

\( S \rightarrow a \mid aA \mid AB \)

\( A \rightarrow a \)

\( B \rightarrow b \)
(iii)

\( S \rightarrow a \mid A \mid aA \)

\( A \rightarrow a \)


Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | June 2012 | Part 3 | Question: 28

For every context free grammar (G) there exists an algorithm that passes any \( w \in L(G) \) in number of steps proportional to



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | June 2013 | Part 3 | Question: 38

A context free grammar for \( L=\{w \mid n_0 (w) > n_1 (w)\} \) is given by:



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | Junet 2015 | Part 3 | Question: 61
options: \( S \rightarrow 0 S \mid 1 S \mid 0 S S \mid 1 S S \mid 0 \mid 1 \)
\( S \rightarrow 0 \mid 0 S \mid 1 S S \mid S 1 S \mid S S 1 \)
questions.correct_answer: \( S \rightarrow 0 \mid 0 S \mid 1 S S \mid S 1 S \mid S S 1 \)
